focus: Extraction engine performance: inter-stage dead time, self-invalidating graph cache (base ref vs work branch), and prompt cache TTL decoupling
rationale: This document is a pure engineering-diagnostics finding (task-437) about the multipass extraction engine's runtime: inter-stage idle time, a self-invalidating graph cache keyed on work-branch tree sha (fixed by reading the base ref instead), and prompt-cache TTL decoupling. It has no positioning/GTM/sales content, so I bypassed PositioningThesis/NarrativeAnchor/Differentiators/FrictionFailure-style business narratives and instead drilled into the architecture/systems regions: ArchitectureOverview and SystemTopology (graph service, navigation-vs-embedded-snapshot, three-tool architecture, five-layer model) since the finding is literally about how the navigator and consult reads hit the graph cache; CoreWorkflows and Primitives/DataIntegrationFlows for the pipeline steps (memory commit → TX → PR) that define what 'pass one' and 'source-spur' mean; DesignTradeoffs and ProcessPolicyUpdates for the prior branch-policy precedents (Obs_ScarletBranchAtRootQuote, Obs_ScarletBranchPolicyQuote, Narrative_ResearchBranchTemporalIsolation, Position_ResearchBranchMethodologyDecision) which are the closest existing positions on 'which branch/ref should a read target' — directly load-bearing for Scarlet's 'the base ref is correct' ruling. ScalabilityPerformance and Position_NavigationDropsContextOrderOfMagnitude/Narrative_NavigationReplacesEmbeddedSnapshot/Narrative_DigestAsRecognitionAid/Narrative_PerspectiveResponseOrderedByVolatility cover the prior navigation-and-caching architecture decisions this finding extends or could revise. Narrative_SynchronousPerMessageIntegration/Obs_AsyncDriftArgumentQuote surface the adjacent sync-vs-async/drift tension relevant to timing and caching tradeoffs. QualityReviewGates, Process, MetricsMonitoring, and DataModelLifecycle were expanded to check for review-timing or metrics positions that might bear on 'measured dev run' framing, but proved only lightly relevant and were not expanded further. I deliberately did not expand the large positioning/pricing/style hubs (Tagline, PackagingPricing, SalesDecks, StyleMetrics, etc.) since nothing in this document touches product narrative, pricing, or voice.

## ^PositioningThesis  [123 children]  ⚠ digest built at 146 children — narration may lag the current graph; expand to ground
The hub traces aswritten's positioning from a developer-first enterprise tool to a layered thesis about expertise installation as a new category of knowledge distribution. The [three-era knowledge framing ^Narrative_TrustedInformationEra] — books, search, curated expertise installation — was crystallized in Mar 2026, with [the category name itself refined ^Claim_CategoryDefinitionCuratedExpertise] by Mar 31 when 'curated' was dropped as redundant. Market targeting shifted from enterprise to [SMB as primary on Mar 12 ^Claim_SMBBiggerThanDevs], then extended to [B2C individuals on Mar 17 ^Narrative_B2CPivot] when both Scarlet and Tony independently converged on solopreneurs and small businesses as the volume play. By May 21 the [tier-continuum resolution ^Narrative_TierContinuumNotDistinctMarkets] reframed Expert/Team/Org as one use case varying only by how many people write to the perspective, holding B2C and enterprise signal simultaneously. The [steering-as-genus frame ^Narrative_SteeringDecisionProcess] (May 26) unified all three use cases: aswritten is always [the memory the steering runs on ^Narrative_AswrittenIsMemorySubstrate], never the loop. Architecture differentiation rests on [flat RAG creating attention dilution ^Claim_ArchitectureGap] versus narrative ontology with conviction levels. The [perspective-vs-information distinction ^Narrative_PerspectiveVsInformation] — modules install how to interpret, not just what to know — anchors the category claim against search and RAG. GTM evolved through [consulting-as-onboarding ^Narrative_ConsultingAsOnboarding], the [side-door GTM pattern ^Insight_SideDoorGTM], and by Jul 15 the [consulting-is-me-using-the-product ^Narrative_ConsultingIsMeUsingProduct] inversion — the client never touches the tool. Fundraising moved from YC Feb rejection (top 10%) through [the enterprise customer as first paying enterprise team ^Claim_EnterpriseCustomerFirstEnterpriseTeamTrial] to a [Jul 2 traction inflection ^Claim_FundraisingTractionInflection_20260625] with a Jul 26 YC reapplication deadline. The [anti-monoculture amplifier frame ^Narrative_AntiMonocultureAmplifier] (Jul 1) and [model-as-OS / perspective-as-application ^Narrative_ModelAsOS] (Jul 2) represent the most recent positioning extensions, with the [CEO context-server reframe ^Narrative_CEOContextServerReframe] and [three demotions ^Narrative_TranscriptDemotionFrictionNotValue] (Jul 15) the latest refinements. The [bounded-domain fidelity argument ^Narrative_BoundedDomainFidelityArgument] (Mar 19) and [matching-search reframe ^Narrative_MatchingSearchReframe] (Apr 24) extend the thesis into expertise modules and practitioner marketplaces respectively.

## ^FrictionFailure  [44 children]  ⚠ digest built at 58 children — narration may lag the current graph; expand to ground
A friction/failure region spanning where AI-accelerated work, sales, onboarding, and internal process all bind. The recurring AI-development pain is volume: code generated faster than humans review it ([back-pressure ^Narrative_AIBackPressureCodeReview], independently named by Scarlet and the customer's chief architect ([convergence ^Claim_AICodeVolumeBottleneck], [Scarlet's PR quote ^Obs_ScarletBackPressureQuote]), and the [action trap ^Narrative_AIActionTrap] where every response prompts another yes ([Tony ^Obs_TonyAITrapQuote]), plus [framing-layer over-resolution ^Narrative_FramingLayerDeflection]. Sales friction clusters around value legibility — [compile blocks sales ^Claim_CompileBlocksSales], [understanding ≠ sale ^Obs_ScarletPurchaseTriggerQuote], [two purchase triggers ^Narrative_TwoPurchaseTriggers], the [activation gap ^Narrative_ActivationGap], [two-sided legibility ^Narrative_TwoSidedLegibilityProblem]. Adoption friction is diagnosed as [contamination fear, not difficulty ^Narrative_TechnicalAdoptionFriction] ([Tony's 10 repos ^Obs_TechnicalChaosQuote], [privacy ^Obs_PrivacyConcernQuote]), plus onboarding gaps — [GitHub opacity ^Obs_OutsideTechBetaUserGitHubOpacityFeedback], [tool-language ^Obs_OutsideTechBetaUserToolLanguageFeedback], [identity grounding ^Narrative_IdentityOnboardingGap], [SMS trust tax ^Narrative_SMSContextDependentTrustTax]. This hub gathers what makes aswritten distinct from generic AI. A third cluster is composability: [module layering ^Narrative_ModuleLayeringComposability] via [multi-repo compilation on one ontology ^Obs_MultiRepoCompilationQuote], [living auto-updating modules ^Narrative_LivingEvolvingModules], and [knowledge packaging ^Narrative_KnowledgePackagingMoment]. ### Narrative_InterviewSaveDraftAnnotatePattern — Interview → Save → Draft → Annotate: The V1 Strategy Session Pattern
witnessed: 20260319
source: Sample_InterviewSaveDraftAnnotate_20260319
The interview elicits thinking not yet in collective memory; save captures it; draft synthesizes it; annotation proves it — the product demonstrates itself through the process
A 9-hour session that produced the V1 strategy document followed a four-phase arc: (1) Interview (~3 hours) — structured questions one at a time, reflect back, pull threads, let the user correct; (2) Save — three memories at natural decision points, extraction pipeline ran while discussion continued; (3) Draft (~4 hours) — section by section against newly grounded material with real-time feedback; (4) Annotate (~2 hours) — full document annotated against collective memory, 97 footnotes with narrative citations, provenance summaries per section. The product demonstrates itself through the process.
Interview → Save → Draft → Annotate: The V1 Strategy Session Pattern
under: CoreWorkflows

### Narrative_OneQuestionAtATime — One Question at a Time: Interview Discipline
witnessed: 20260319
source: Sample_InterviewSaveDraftAnnotate_20260319
One question at a time — listen, reflect back, pull the thread — is the discipline that makes the interview format work
Do not dump 10 questions. Ask one, listen, reflect back, pull the thread. Name patterns without prescribing: 'Here's what I'm hearing' not 'here's what you should do.' The interview format is what makes the strategy emerge from the user's thinking rather than the worldview alone.
One Question at a Time: Interview Discipline
under: CoreWorkflows

### Narrative_ReviewLoopIsRemember — Review Is Just Another Remember: The Natural Loop
type: Narrative
witnessed: 20250714
source: Sample_DesignSession_20260331
Review corrections are just another remember — the review loop is the same pipeline recursively applied, not a special flow
Review corrections produce a new memory, which triggers extraction, which produces new transactions. The loop is natural: remember → extraction → review → corrections? → remember (review memory) → extraction → review → ... → done. Scarlet: 'Any review does is produce another memory, right? It's not a special type of memory; it may have a specific type of content in it because it's a review, but it's just another memory.' Each remember in the chain commits to the same location. In Team + GitHub, the PR accumulates all rounds. Merge is offered at the end but never automatic. Authority to merge is a GitHub concern, not an aswritten concern. No automated consensus — humans decide when they're done.
Review Is Just Another Remember: The Natural Loop
under: CoreWorkflows

### Narrative_SaveBeforeDrafting — Save Before Drafting: Ground the Knowledge Before Synthesizing It
witnessed: 20260319
source: Sample_InterviewSaveDraftAnnotate_20260319
Save memories at natural decision points before drafting — the extraction pipeline needs time, and grounded knowledge produces grounded documents
Three memories were saved before the document was started. By the time sections were being drafted, the extraction pipeline had run and the annotation tool could verify claims against the new knowledge. The document was grounded because the knowledge was grounded first. Sequence: Save → wait for extraction → compile → draft → annotate.
Save Before Drafting: Ground the Knowledge Before Synthesizing It
under: CoreWorkflows

### Narrative_ContentCommentarySplit
type: Narrative
witnessed: 20260701
source: Sample_SelfServeProductSurface_20260624
The keystone design decision of the session. Current memories conflate primary source content with commentary. The correct model: per-message integration captures primary source material (content layer); reconciliation is authored commentary about a referent set of previously extracted nodes (commentary layer). The read model already separates these (digest renders grounded position + RECONCILED conclusion); the write practice has not yet implemented the split. This is a write primitive that is read-implemented but not yet written.
Content/commentary split: per-message integration is the content layer; reconciliation is the authored commentary layer

### Narrative_ReconciliationAsSetTransaction
type: Narrative
witnessed: 20260701
source: Sample_SelfServeProductSurface_20260624
Reconciliation is structurally a transaction about a set of transactions or a set of nodes in the graph. This is exactly what correction and commentary about the past are. The product surface: select multiple transactions, say something about them, submit a reconciliation request. The system asks 'are you talking about these?' — surfacing which graph nodes it is reasoning from. This is the legibility differentiator: unlike current AI use where the user never knows what the AI is reasoning from, aswritten surfaces the referent set explicitly. Disambiguate, never permission — the prompt rule.
Reconciliation is a transaction about a set of transactions or nodes — the design of correction and commentary about the past

### Position_AbsenceIsNoRecognizedAssociation
type: Position
witnessed: 20260616
conviction: decision (origin, 2026-06-16)
source: Sample_DigestRecognitionAid_20260616
Absence is no recognized association, never nothing here. Targeted grounding may trust recognized-absence; creative exploration deliberately distrusts it and expands past the recognized. Creative reading costs expansion by design. A cheap legible-absence is dangerous precisely because the encounters it lets you skip are where novelty would have struck. Mitigation: honesty about scope (recognition aid, never account of meaning) and the always-reachable store.
under: Narrative_DigestAsRecognitionAid

### Position_ExpansionIsSteering
type: Position
witnessed: 20260616
conviction: decision (origin, 2026-06-16)
source: Sample_DigestRecognitionAid_20260616
Expansion is steering, not just quoting. Expanding a subtree you genuinely lean on is the product working: you get the trajectory and variance, not just a verbatim quote. The bad case is expanding to discover whether anything is there; the good case is expanding onto material you have already decided is relevant. Route to the subtree, not the child.
under: Narrative_DigestAsRecognitionAid

### Position_IndexNeverGatesStore
type: Position
witnessed: 20260616
conviction: principle (origin, 2026-06-16)
source: Sample_DigestRecognitionAid_20260616
The index must never gate the store. Steering requires compression but compression via fixed themes is exactly the reductionism the discourse-act ontology exists to refuse. A claim is not about something; it is available to be read as many things. The digest does not have to anticipate the novel reading. It has to get you close enough via an old association that you expand and encounter the material.
under: Narrative_DigestAsRecognitionAid

### Position_MultiplicityInVariation
type: Position
witnessed: 20260616
conviction: principle (origin, 2026-06-16)
source: Sample_DigestRecognitionAid_20260616
Multiplicity lives in variation, not a theme label. The digest already refuses single-theme reduction via the variation block: it surfaces reads as X, and as Y, and the road not taken. That is the anti-reductionist mechanism and it stays load-bearing.

### Position_SortVsRankTensionResolved
type: Position
witnessed: 20260701
conviction: decision (origin, 2026-07-01)
The ordering decision dissolved the long-standing sort-vs-rank tension in the compression-only projection principle. Order is unavoidable — hubs emit in some sequence — but under this decision the order is a fixed property of the graph state, not a per-call judgment. The projection still never suppresses, promotes, or hides availability; focus adaptivity lives entirely in an appended section.
